subject: Mathematics
overall_learning_objectives: Construct a line parallel to a given line through an external point
Apply the SSS (Side-Side-Side) triangle construction criterion
Apply the SAS (Side-Angle-Side) triangle construction criterion
Understand why some shapes tile the plane and others don't
Identify the 'Unit Tile' in a repeating pattern

lesson_plan.title: Constructing Parallel Lines
topics_covered: Steps to copy an angle using a compass
student_activities: Construct line 'm' parallel to 'l' at a distance of 4 cm
Identify transversal lines in their construction

lesson_plan.title: Building Triangles (SSS & SAS)
topics_covered: Drawing triangles given 2 sides and 1 included angle
teaching_strategy: Logic check: 'Can we build a triangle with sides 2, 3, 6?'
student_activities: Construct $\triangle PQR$ where $PQ=4, QR=5,$ and $\angle Q=60^{\circ}$

teacher_tip: When constructing an SSS triangle, always start with the longest side as the base—it makes the arcs easier to draw and see.
